$comment: Reference case for inverse_square_falloff (atten = 1 / (constant + linear*d + quadratic*d^2)). Three free coefficients — the most multi-parameter fit in the reference set to date. Shape is a rational function; LM has to resolve the constant/linear/quadratic contributions simultaneously from a single-variable sweep.
notes: Coefficients match the OpenGL standard attenuation defaults. 40-point sweep spans the near-light high-attenuation region (d=0.1) through the quadratic-dominated tail (d=20). Linear / quadratic tolerances at 5 % are a bit loose because the two terms partially alias for intermediate distances — that mutual information is what a real regression in LM's damping strategy would perturb.
